calendar.matters:
Defendant Richard Wagner'S Motion To Dismiss
Off Calendar Dec-10-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Bruce A.Hatkoff, Esq., Hatkoff & Minassian, A Law Corproation, 818-990-5180, for Plaintiff;
Natalia A. Minassian, Esq., Hatkoff & Minassian, A Law Corproation, 818-990-5180, for Plaintiff;
Anthony Flores, Esq., Telephone 415-235-3652, appearing for Defendant Richard Wagner;

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court rules as follows:

Defendant Richard Wagner'S Motion To Dismiss is denied as being untimely and is waived by CCP Section 418.10(e)(3) because it was filed after defendant's answer and in the alternative the 3 year statute was tolled after notice of levy was filed. Prevailing/Moving party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/HEK)

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Dec-11-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Natalia A. Minassian of the law offices of Jonathan Neil & Associates (818-990-5180) appears by telephone as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Anthopny Flores of the law offices of Anthony Flores (415-235-3652) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

Richard Wagner, represented defendant/moving party is present.

Clerk administers witness oath to defendant, Richard Wagner. Defendant is examined by the Court and the cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

DEFENDANT RICHARD WAGNER'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT and DEFAULT JUDGMENT is GRANTED on the condition that the money held remains in escrow. There shall be no disbursement except by agreement of the parties and/or further order of court. By the end of business today, Defendant, Mr. Wagner or his attorney shall send to plaintiff counsel documentation, evidence as to what was held by the escrow company. The moneys that have already been collected by plaintiff, either through a bank or any other source shall remain with the plaintiff. Mr. Wagner may file a cross-claim if necessary. Request for terminating sanctions is denied. A response to the complaint is to be filed and served within 20 days. No motion quash is allowed. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR No. 11185, e-mail: adelgado@sftc.org (L&M302)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-01-2015 Off Calendar

Notice Of Scheduling Default Prove Up Hearing
Off Calendar Jul-28-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Charles Crompton, Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos, CSR: Mitch Lyon, #6848

Jill Bates-Moore, Esq., specially appearing for Plaintiff, JONATHAN NEIL & ASSOCIATES, INC., a California corporation.
No appearance by defaulted defendant(s) RICHARD WAGNER, aka RICHARD J. WAGNER, aka RICHARD JOHN WAGNER, individually and dba GREEN ST. AUTOBODY & REPAIRS, aka GREEN STREET AUTO BODY & REPAIR.
Default of Defendant(s) has been duly entered.
Christine Lam sworn and examinded on behalf of Plaintiff.
After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff and against Defaulted Defendant(s) as follows:

Damages: $39,901.50
Pre-Judgment Interest: $4,285.31
Attorney's Fees: $9,975.40
Costs: $516.00
Total Judgment in the amount of $54,678.21
7. OTHER: Plaintiff is entitled to the immediate possession of the security described in attachment "A" and net proceeds received from the sale of such security will be credited against the judgment amount.
Judgment signed in open court.
On order of the court on the record the clerk cancelled the Original Promissory Note. Original cancelled Promissory Note was returned to cousel for the care and custody of the court.
